spectrum.digitalWindowEnable: Switch to a fixed view and lock to the most suitable 5 kHz digital window
spectrum.digitalWindowDisable: Currently fixed to the 5 kHz digital window. Click to return to follow view
spectrum.openwebrxDetailEnable: Switch to the OpenWebRX digital detail spectrum view
spectrum.openwebrxDetailDisable: Currently in the OpenWebRX digital detail spectrum view. Click to return to the full spectrum

system.title: System Settings
system.allowPublicViewingOnDesc: Unauthenticated users can view as observers, but cannot operate
system.allowPublicViewingOffDesc: Unauthenticated users must log in with a token to access
system.realtimeSettingsCardDesc: This card unifies the realtime audio path. Browsers normally reuse the current site's /livekit entrypoint, and only special deployments need an advanced override URL.
system.realtimeSettingsSimpleGuide: Recommended flow: keep the default same-origin /livekit entrypoint first, then choose auto or compatibility mode. Open advanced override and diagnostics only for dedicated domains, extra reverse proxies, or non-standard paths.
system.realtimeLiveKitBenefitsTitle: Why LiveKit is recommended first: it is usually more resilient for public, HTTPS, weak-network, and intermittently unstable realtime voice, with better browser compatibility and recovery behavior.
system.realtimeLiveKitBenefitsDesc: When the network jitters or briefly drops and reconnects, LiveKit is usually less likely than compatibility mode to produce audible interruptions. If you mainly need local testing, LAN use, single-port deployment, or the lowest possible local latency, compatibility mode (WS/PCM) can still be the better fit.
system.realtimeBrowserEntryDesc: By default, clients reuse the current site's /livekit path. You no longer need to remember or expose port 7880 to browsers.
system.realtimeBrowserEntryCurrentLabel: Realtime voice entrypoint currently exposed to clients
system.liveKitPublicUrlDesc: Usually leave this empty. Only set it when you use a dedicated domain, an extra reverse proxy layer, a non-standard path, or when the current site's /livekit route cannot reach LiveKit signaling directly.
system.liveKitPublicUrlAutoHint: When empty, the app uses the current site's /livekit path as the LiveKit signaling entrypoint. This field is ignored when compatibility mode is forced.
system.liveKitPublicUrlManualHint: Advanced override enabled. After saving, all clients using the LiveKit primary path will use this URL first.
system.liveKitMacInstallHint: To enable the LiveKit primary path on the macOS desktop app, install livekit-server locally first:
system.liveKitMacFallbackHint: If it is not installed, the app will automatically fall back to ws compatibility mode so listening and voice still work.
system.realtimeTransportPolicyDesc: Auto mode prefers LiveKit as the primary path and is usually more resilient on public, weak, or complex networks. If the server or client cannot establish the LiveKit audio path, the app automatically falls back to the single-port WebSocket compatibility transport. Compatibility mode is better suited to deployments that can only expose one port, LAN/local testing, or cases where the lowest possible local latency matters most.
system.realtimeTransportPolicyCompat: Compatibility Mode (Single Port / Low Local Latency)
system.realtimeTransportPolicyAutoHint: Default choice. Prefer the LiveKit primary path and automatically fall back to the single-port compatibility transport when needed.
system.realtimeTransportPolicyCompatHint: Best for single-port deployments, LAN/local testing, or when the lowest local latency matters more than LiveKit's network resilience. LiveKit will be skipped and the single-port WebSocket compatibility transport will always be used.
system.realtimeAdminGuideDesc: This section consolidates advanced overrides, deployment requirements, the server's current recommended path, and the most useful clues for diagnosing LiveKit failures.
system.realtimeCurrentPathHint: This is the server's recommended path based on current configuration and bridge health. If a client cannot establish LiveKit, it will automatically fall back to compatibility mode.
system.realtimeNoRecentIssue: No recent LiveKit bridge issue is currently recorded.
system.realtimePortRequirementsDesc: Browsers normally enter signaling through the current site's /livekit path. For deployment, prioritize the site entrypoint plus the RTC TCP and UDP media ports. If that is not possible, the app can fall back to the single-port compatibility transport.
system.realtimeEffectiveUrlLabel: LiveKit URL currently exposed to clients
system.realtimePortHintAuto: No URL is usually required. Browsers will use the current site's /livekit path as the LiveKit signaling entrypoint.
system.realtimePortHintCompat: Compatibility mode does not require extra LiveKit ports. It reuses the site's existing single entrypoint.
system.realtimePortHintFallback: Avoid exposing port 7880 publicly by default. If 7881 or the UDP range cannot be exposed, prefer compatibility mode or allow automatic fallback.
system.realtimeCredentialMissingHint: No usable LiveKit credential file or explicit override was detected.
system.realtimeCredentialLinuxHint: On Linux servers, run sudo tx5dr livekit-creds rotate to rotate credentials and restart services immediately.
system.realtimeFallbackDialogUnknownIssue: The realtime primary path is currently unavailable.
system.realtimeFallbackDialogDescription: The realtime primary path could not be established. You can switch to compatibility mode (WS/PCM) immediately and continue the current action without changing the global policy in Settings.
system.realtimeFallbackDialogActionDescription: When you click the button below, only this connection attempt will skip LiveKit and retry through compatibility mode (WS/PCM).
system.realtimeFallbackDialogRetryTimeout: The compatibility retry timed out. Check whether the current site entrypoint and /api/realtime/ws-compat are reachable.
system.desktopHttpsDesc: Adds a browser/LAN entrypoint for the Electron desktop app. The desktop window itself does not depend on this, but browser access with realtime voice usually needs HTTPS.
system.desktopHttpsPurpose: When do you need this: if you want a phone, tablet, or another computer to open this desktop app in a browser and use realtime voice or microphone features, enabling HTTPS is recommended.
system.desktopHttpsScenarioDesktopTitle: Desktop app only on this machine
system.desktopHttpsScenarioDesktopDesc: You usually do not need HTTPS here. The desktop window can keep working as-is.
system.desktopHttpsScenarioLanDesc: HTTPS is recommended. You can generate a self-signed certificate in one click and manually continue past the browser warning on first visit.
system.desktopHttpsScenarioPublicDesc: Prepare a domain and a properly signed certificate, then import the PEM certificate and private key to avoid browser security warnings.
system.desktopHttpsEnable: Enable HTTPS for browser / LAN access
system.desktopHttpsEnableDesc: Turn this on only when other devices should open this desktop app in a browser and need realtime voice or microphone access.
system.desktopHttpsModeSelfSignedDesc: Best for personal, LAN, or temporary access. Browsers will warn that the certificate is untrusted and you must continue manually.
system.desktopHttpsModeImportedDesc: Best for public, domain-based, or multi-user access. Prepare a PEM certificate file and its matching PEM private key.
system.desktopHttpsRedirect: Automatically redirect external HTTP access to HTTPS
system.desktopHttpsStatusDesc: Shows the actual browser entry URL, current certificate status, and the LAN addresses you can share right now.
system.desktopHttpsNoCertificate: No usable certificate yet. Generate a self-signed certificate first, or import a proper certificate.
system.desktopHttpsAccessUrlPending: Shown after you save and enable it
system.desktopHttpsLanHint: Once enabled, both local and LAN access URLs will be shown here.
system.desktopHttpsSelfSignedLimitations: Self-signed limitations: browsers will show an "unsafe" or "untrusted certificate" warning; every device must manually continue on first visit; this is better for personal, internal, or testing use, not for long-term public access.
system.desktopHttpsImportedHint: Proper certificates are recommended for domain-based and public access. In v1, you manually import a PEM certificate and PEM private key; the app does not request Let's Encrypt certificates automatically.
system.desktopHttpsSelectKeyTitle: Select the PEM private key file
system.decodeWhileTransmittingOffDesc: Stop decoding when any operator is transmitting, avoids residual signals
system.decodeWhileTransmittingOnDesc: Continue decoding during TX cycle, supports dual-cycle remote TX/RX
system.spectrumWhileTransmittingOnDesc: Continue displaying spectrum while transmitting, monitor TX signal in real time
system.spectrumWhileTransmittingOffDesc: Pause spectrum analysis while transmitting, reduces CPU usage
system.receiverInfoDesc: If left empty, the first operator's callsign and grid will be used automatically
system.pskrDesc: Automatically report decoded FT8/FT4 signals to
system.decodeWindowDesc: Control the number and timing of decode passes per slot. More windows improve weak signal reception but increase CPU load.
system.closeBehaviorDesc: Default behavior when closing the main window (desktop app only)
system.desktopUpdateDesc: Checks whether a newer build exists and provides the download link. The app does not download or install updates automatically.
system.desktopUpdateDownloadOptionsDesc: Each release can provide installer and archive packages. Choose the one that matches how you use the app.
system.desktopUpdateOpenFailed: Failed to open the download link.

freqPresets.title: Frequency Preset Management
freqPresets.description: Customize the preset frequencies shown in the frequency selector. Add frequently used frequencies or remove bands you don't use.
freqPresets.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore the default standard frequency presets? Your custom presets will be overwritten.
freqPresets.empty: Preset list is empty. Please add at least one frequency preset.
freqPresets.duplicate: A preset with this frequency already exists
freqPresets.unknownBand: Cannot infer an amateur band from this frequency

remoteAccess.title: Network Access
remoteAccess.description: Access from other devices via browser
remoteAccess.sameNetworkHint: Ensure devices are on the same LAN
